summary: Reporter says MAUI on iOS shows a white canvas on .NET 10 with SkiaSharp 3.116.0 while similar code works on .NET 9, and logs include 'Failed to bind EAGLDrawable' framebuffer errors. The project is Mapsui MapControl and includes paint handlers for both GL and non-GL surfaces. Last known good version provided is 2.88.9.

bugSignals.stackTrace: Failed to bind EAGLDrawable: <CAEAGLLayer ...> to GL_RENDERBUFFER 1\nFailed to make complete multisample framebuffer object 8cd6\nFailed to make complete framebuffer object 8cd6

stepsToReproduce: Run a MAUI iOS app on .NET 10 using the GL paint path from Mapsui MapControl.
Trigger rendering for the map control.
Observe white canvas and EAGL drawable/framebuffer errors in iOS logs.
codeSnippets: private void OnGLPaintSurface(object? sender, SKPaintGLSurfaceEventArgs args) { if (_glView?.GRContext is null) return; PaintSurface(args.Surface.Canvas); }
private void OnPaintSurface(object? sender, SKPaintSurfaceEventArgs args) { PaintSurface(args.Surface.Canvas); }
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: MAUI on iOS, .NET 10 (fails), .NET 9 (reported working), SkiaSharp 3.116.0, Visual Studio for Mac, Mapsui MapControl

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: Current iOS MAUI GL handler still routes through deprecated iOS OpenGL/EAGL code paths.

regression.reason: Reporter claims .NET 9 works while .NET 10 fails, but the underlying iOS OpenGL path is deprecated and fragile; this may be environment/runtime regression rather than a recent SkiaSharp code change.

analysis.summary: The issue maps to the iOS MAUI GL rendering path. The iOS SKGLView handler is marked obsolete for iOS 12+ and still uses SKGLView, whose initialization creates an EAGL OpenGLES2 context. The reported errors are emitted when CAEAGLLayer binding/renderbuffer setup fails, which matches this code path. In contrast, the Mac Catalyst handler already uses a Metal view, indicating a modern backend path exists in the codebase.
analysis.rationale: This is a bug because expected rendering fails and the reporter provides concrete runtime errors. The area is SkiaSharp.Views.Maui because the failing path is in MAUI handlers, not core drawing APIs. iOS and OpenGL labels are supported by the EAGL/GLK usage in source, and compatibility is relevant because the report is tied to .NET 10/iOS behavior changes on a deprecated API stack.

keySignals.text: Failed to bind EAGLDrawable ... Failed to make complete framebuffer object 8cd6
keySignals.interpretation: Failure occurs in iOS OpenGL drawable/framebuffer setup before successful rendering.
Indicates runtime/platform compatibility change across framework versions.
Current iOS GL handler explicitly warns this path is deprecated.

codeInvestigation.finding: iOS handler is marked obsolete and still binds ISKGLView to SKGLView.
Initialize() creates EAGL OpenGLES2 context and GLK drawable formats, matching the EAGLDrawable failure signature.
Mac Catalyst maps ISKGLView to SKMetalView, showing an existing Metal handler pattern.
Apple canvas handler uses SKCanvasView path that does not depend on EAGL drawable binding.

workarounds: Use the CPU canvas rendering control instead of the GL rendering control on iOS while this is investigated.
Prefer the Metal-backed rendering path where available.
nextQuestions: Which exact iOS version/device reproduces this most consistently?
Does a minimal MAUI app without Mapsui reproduce the same EAGL drawable errors?
Is failure limited to .NET 10, or also reproducible on current .NET 9 workloads with same app path?

resolution.hypothesis: Deprecated iOS OpenGL/EAGL path used by the MAUI GL handler is failing to create a valid drawable/framebuffer on the reporter environment.

proposals.title: Switch affected app path to CPU canvas rendering
proposals.description: As a short-term workaround on iOS, use the non-GL canvas rendering path to avoid EAGL drawable initialization.

proposals.title: Migrate iOS MAUI GL handler to Metal backend
proposals.description: Align iOS handler behavior with existing Mac Catalyst Metal-based handler to avoid deprecated OpenGL stack.

proposals.title: Collect focused matrix repro
proposals.description: Capture minimal repro across framework and iOS versions to separate runtime regression from handler-specific behavior.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Switch affected app path to CPU canvas rendering
resolution.recommendedReason: Most immediate mitigation with low implementation effort while maintainers investigate a Metal migration.

actionability.reason: Behavior is broken with concrete platform errors and likely needs handler/backend work rather than user-only configuration changes.

missingInfo: Exact iOS version and device model
Confirmation whether CPU canvas path restores rendering on .NET 10

actions.description: Ensure labels include backend/OpenGL, tenet/compatibility, and partner/maui alongside existing bug/area/os labels.
Link prior iOS EAGL drawable issue for historical context.
Acknowledge issue, share immediate workaround direction, and request minimal environment details.
actions.comment: Thanks for the detailed report and error logs. This looks like a failure in the iOS OpenGL drawable setup path under the .NET 10 environment, which explains the white canvas behavior. As a short-term workaround while we investigate, please use the non-GL canvas rendering path on iOS for this control. Could you also share the exact iOS version/device and whether a minimal MAUI sample (without Mapsui) reproduces the same error?
